title: Project Redwood
excerpt: Developing a global loyalty roadmap unifying 14 country programs into a single coalition framework.
challenge: Fourteen country programs, four loyalty engines, and a fragmented customer data model that made coalition impossible.
approach: We ran a 12-week diagnostic across all 14 markets, designed a unified currency and tier construct, and built a phased rollout sequenced by data readiness rather than market size.
testimonial: QBF Consulting reframed our entire loyalty operating model. Twelve months on, every KPI we set has been beaten.

title: The Atlas Program
excerpt: Designing and launching a tech-first airline loyalty program from a blank sheet, including engine selection and partner ecosystem.
challenge: Greenfield program with a 14-month deadline, no existing customer data infrastructure, and a board mandate to launch ahead of a competitor relaunch.
approach: We owned the program design, vendor selection, partner negotiation, and operational readiness. A parallel-track approach let strategy, tech, and operations move in lockstep rather than sequence.
testimonial: The QBF team operated as a partner, not a vendor. Atlas launched on time, on budget, and ahead of every competitive milestone.

title: Echo One
excerpt: Ongoing optimisation and performance management for an 8M-member luxury hospitality program.
challenge: Stagnant engagement, rising redemption costs, and an executive team unable to defend the program economics in a downturn.
approach: We embedded a four-person team running quarterly performance reviews, monthly campaign optimisation, and a rebuilt P&L that connected every loyalty action to incremental revenue.
testimonial: Echo One stopped being a marketing line and became a P&L. That single shift saved the program through last year's downturn.

title: Horizon Coalition
excerpt: Designing a 12-brand coalition currency for a sovereign-backed retail group across UAE and Saudi Arabia.
challenge: Twelve operating brands, twelve P&Ls, and a board demanding a unified currency without cannibalising any single brand's program.
approach: We ran a 16-week design sprint with each brand's leadership, modelled cross-brand redemption economics, and shipped a coalition rulebook that protected single-brand value while unlocking incremental margin.
testimonial: We had tried coalition twice before. QBF was the first team to make the economics actually work.

title: Northstar Tech Evaluation
excerpt: An objective evaluation of nine loyalty platforms culminating in a board-ready recommendation and migration plan.
challenge: Five-year-old loyalty stack creaking under modern personalisation demands, with internal advocates split across three vendors.
approach: We ran a structured RFP with weighted scoring across architecture, data, AI, and TCO. Live load tests separated demoware from production-ready platforms.
testimonial: QBF's evaluation gave us the only clear-eyed recommendation we received. The board approved on the first read.

title: Meridian Academy
excerpt: Building an internal loyalty capability program for 140 marketing leaders across six markets.
challenge: Loyalty practice scattered across six markets with no shared vocabulary, frameworks, or measurement standards.
approach: We delivered a CLMP-anchored capability program, custom for the bank's regulatory context, with live cohort coaching and a certified internal trainer pipeline.
testimonial: We finally have a single loyalty language across the group. That alone has saved us months on every regional initiative.
testimonialAuthor: Group Head of Customer, Meridian Bank
